content.problemStatement: Write a program to check whether a number given as user input is positive, negative, or zero.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 100000), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (−10^9 ≤ N ≤ 10^9).
content.outputFormat:
For each test case output on a separate line one of the strings:
"Positive" if N > 0,
"Negative" if N < 0,
"Zero" if N == 0.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is one test case. The number 20 is greater than zero, so the program prints "Positive".
The single input number is 0, therefore the output is "Zero".
-95 is less than zero, so the program prints "Negative".

solution.approach: For each test case read the integer N and compare it with zero. If N is greater than zero print "Positive", if it is less than zero print "Negative", otherwise print "Zero". The algorithm is O(1) per test case, resulting in O(T) total time and O(1) auxiliary space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used regardless of T.
